issues.title: SEC-2b.7: Causal inference overstated + scoring criterion needs citation
issues.description: SEC-2b.7 (SEC-2b.7-rewrite.c2.json): (1) Paragraph states scoreboard was 'removed approximately one day after our poller flagged its drift' — this conflates correlation with causation. Separate factual claim (endpoint returns 404) from causal inference. (2) The 'direction_correct AND snr_sufficient' scoring criterion is stated as fact without citation from the dome's code. Source: SEC-2b.7-rewrite.c2.json.
issues.fix_details: Both concerns resolved in current SEC-2b.7: (1) causal inference is now neutral framing with precise timestamps and 13-hour removal window; (2) scoring criterion cites ISS-705-verification.json. Supplementary: 404 is GitHub Pages routing artifact, file still exists in repo main branch at 200 via raw.githubusercontent.com. Analyst confirmed via proposal-ISS-802-resolution.json.

issues.title: SEC-2.4: 52% angular diameter reduction mislabeled as mid-afternoon (should be late afternoon)
issues.description: Section 7.10 (SEC-2.4): The summary sentence '52% reduction in angular diameter by mid-afternoon' mislabels the calculation endpoint. The 52% figure is computed at a 90° hour angle = 6 hours from noon = 6 PM (late afternoon), not mid-afternoon (2-4 PM). The preceding sentence correctly labels the 90° case as 'late afternoon'. The summary sentence needs to match. TLDR may also need checking. Source: SEC-2.4.c2 curmudgeon review.

issues.title: SEC-8.11: 'homepage advertises -17 to -21 nT' is unverifiable from captured raw-text
issues.description: SEC-8.11 body says 'The homepage advertises −17 to −21 nT at Ebro' but this specific range does not appear in captured raw-text files (01-home.txt, 03-predictions.txt). The range IS derivable from the dome's own predictions: using Ebro's station-specific FSF (1.029): −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.029 = −17.8 nT, and W004 pred = −21.7 nT for Ebro. The text already makes this derivation argument. Fix: replace 'The homepage advertises' with 'The dome's eclipse predictions for Ebro span' or add a parenthetical showing how the range is derived. Source: SEC-8.11.c1 curmudgeon review.

WIN-058b detail_evidence: r=0.96 vs r=0.992 inconsistency argument (PRED-KAPPA-001 vs Exhibit A) has an unaddressed dome defender response — defender can argue the two r-values measure different statistics. Text should clarify what each r-value measures or note that the dome provides no specification of what each measures (which is already present). Needs minor prose strengthening.

PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning and tldr: fabricated author citation "Rosales-Linares et al. (2019)" — no such author exists in Schumann resonance literature. Correct author: Pazos et al. (2019). Also wrong DOI: 10.1016/j.jastp.2018.08.006 should be 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091. Mischaracterized finding: "all five G2-G4 events" should be "averaged amplitude across five 14-day storm periods". And "bug or cherry-pick" binary should be a trichotomy (also: stale dashboard text).

SEC-1.5 (part1) still says "at the equatorial ring (r = 14,105 km), n ≈ 1.83" — stale value from before ISS-855 fix. Sections 1.8 and 1.9 both say r ≈ 10,008 km, n = 1.44. This internal inconsistency contradicts our own cross-section statements and hands dome defender a ready attack: "Your refraction sections disagree on where the equator is."

SEC-2.3 (part2) puts "unidirectional dielectric acceleration" in quotation marks attributing it to the dome model, but this exact phrase does not appear in any dome page (raw-text). The actual dome phrasing is "dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation" (Domain 4). Fabricated quoted attribution undermines our credibility.

Part 5 intro paragraph says "six binary tests" but the Verdict Summary table has SEVEN rows (dome declared six; we added Sydney-Eratosthenes). Text contradicts the actual section content visible immediately below it.

Part 1 scorecard sublabel says "None produces a prediction that both derives from dome geometry AND differs from standard physics" — contradicts own review data. Under the natural reading of code_analysis tags, WIN-044 and WIN-057 are tagged derives_from_dome:true. The hardcoded 0 count is not supported by the criterion as written. Curmudgeon recommends: add "confirmed by measurement" as a third condition, making "None derives from dome geometry, differs from standard physics, AND is confirmed by measurement" — which IS actually 0.

SEC-7.1 (part7) Antarctic rim-circumnavigation paragraph presents both 125,760 km (rim) and ~112,000 km (70°S) figures then asserts "factor-of-eight discrepancy" — ambiguous whether this refers to 125,760/13,700≈9.2 or 112,000/13,700≈8.2. Cleaner to pick one comparison. Also: ship list (Soviet Ob, HMS Endurance) weakly supports the circumnavigation claim with these sources; curmudgeon recommends more precise references.

KILLSHOT GAIA table Betelgeuse row: attributed to "Hipparcos (van Leeuwen 2007)" but the 4.51 ± 0.80 mas value is NOT van Leeuwen 2007 (which gives 6.55 ± 0.83 mas). Correct source is Harper et al. 2017 (VLA-Hipparcos combined). Also: Lauer et al. 2020 citation links to a NASA press release, not the peer-reviewed paper (Lauer et al. 2025, AJ 170, 22).

SEC-7.1 Antarctic circumnavigation paragraph: (1) v22 quote over-loaded — dome v22 log says "Southern arc distances 2x too long" about cross-equatorial formulas not specifically circumnavigation; (2) refraction rebuttal misses AE projection assumption (not stated). TLDR now patched (2026-04-14) to include circumnavigation argument. Remaining: verify v22 quote applicability and AE projection note.
issues.fix_details: EXP-161 integrated: paragraph 4 of SEC-7.1 replaced. v22 quote scoped to cross-equatorial coord-formula errors; AE projection derivation r=20,015×160/180=17,791 km shown inline; Finsler-metric defense pre-empted.

issues.description: W019 (NMP poleward drift) verdict should be "falsified" not "standard_physics". The dome made a specific directional claim (poleward drift DOMINATES), observations show lateral drift is 5.5x poleward, and the dome author himself marked it REFINED (concession of failure). Standard_physics is too charitable — it implies the phenomenon is already explained by standard physics without capturing that the dome's specific directional prediction failed on its own terms.
issues.fix_details: Verdict change already applied by human approval commit a05ebca (2026-04-14T06:32). Issue pre-dated the applied change.

issues.description: W020 (SAA-Roaring40s anomaly ≥3%) verdict should be "falsified" not "unfalsifiable". The prediction specified a concrete ≥3% geopotential height anomaly threshold. The author marked it REFINED and conceded the effect is smaller than predicted. A specific quantitative prediction that fails its own threshold IS falsified. The detail_reasoning says "no clear test criterion" which contradicts the ≥3% criterion in the prediction itself.
issues.fix_details: Verdict change already applied by human approval commit a05ebca (2026-04-14T06:32). Issue pre-dated the applied change.

issues.description: PRED-NEW-002 (aetheric gravity wave 9.2 km/s) verdict should be "standard_physics" not "unfalsifiable". The value 9.2 km/s falls within the observed P-wave velocity range in the upper mantle (8-13 km/s). The claim IS testable and matches standard seismology. Calling it unfalsifiable gives the dome an unearned rhetorical advantage — they can say "you admit it can't be tested!" when reality is worse for them (it CAN be tested and IS just relabeled seismology).
issues.fix_details: Verdict change already applied by human approval commit a05ebca (2026-04-14T06:32). Issue pre-dated the applied change.

Should acknowledge this to strengthen argument: radial n(r) produces refraction primarily for oblique rays with large horizontal displacement — eclipse shadow paths uniquely satisfy this.

issues.description: SEC-7.1 Antarctic paragraph cites Soviet Ob (1956-57), HMS Endurance, and modern Southern Ocean yacht races as confirming ~13,700 km figure via 70°S circumnavigation. None of these actually performed full 70°S circumnavigations: Soviet Ob did coastal work for Mirny resupply; HMS Endurance patrols South Georgia/Peninsula sector; modern yacht races (Vendée Globe, Volvo, Jules Verne) run at 40-55°S due to sea ice. The actual basis for the 13,700 km figure is coastline cartography + satellite altimetry, NOT vessel circumnavigation. Recommended fix: replace ship citations with SCAR ADD / IBCSO / Bellingshausen 1819-21 (canonical first Antarctic circumnavigation, at higher latitudes/non-continuous 70°S track). Out-of-scope from EXP-161 which limited itself to v22 quote and AE projection.
issues.fix_details: Current sections.json does not contain Soviet Ob, HMS Endurance, or yacht race references. EXP-161 integration (commit abea10f) replaced ship citations with satellite altimetry and GPS-tracked circumnavigation records. ISS-996 stale — already resolved.
issues.notes: Filed by human after noticing decider report flagged this for follow-up but never created an issue. The pipeline gap: "out of scope, tracked for follow-up" comments in decider reports do not actually create tracked work items.

issues.description: WIN-010 detail_verdict_text missing cross-reference to WIN-025/WIN-068 triple-counting context. The same ionospheric Sq mechanism underlies all three (WIN-010/025/068). Curmudgeon c4 recommends adding: WIN-010 is one of three entries for the same mechanism — relevant when dome cites confirmed prediction count.
WIN-010 detail_claim missing temporal anchor. Should include 17:20 UTC so Chapman argument (timing tracks eclipse shadow) can be checked against claim.
WIN-010 tldr_verdict last sentence uses vague "calibration circular" phrasing. Should say "training data serving as its own test" to be precise.

WIN-029 detail_evidence describes the dome cavity height as H_eff ≈ 3,400 km "area-weighted average". Curmudgeon c3 notes this should be "linear radial average" — the averaging method matters for the 22 Hz vs 35 Hz discrepancy argument.

WIN-029 symmetry defense not preempted. After citing Sentman 1990 and Mushtak-Williams 2002 corrections, text does not note that those corrections use independently measured ionospheric conductivity (no free parameters), unlike the dome's 26% aetheric damping which is tuned. This asymmetry is the key defense against dome's "both models need corrections" argument.

WIN-029 detail_verdict_text: "algebraically equivalent to a standard-model derivation" is misleading. It should say the derivation algebraically inverts a textbook quarter-wave formula to back-solve a dome length from the known measured frequency.

WIN-029 tldr_evidence conflates two distinct percent-discrepancies. The 5.3% is the dome's claimed agreement; the 10.8% is our counter-calculation from dome's own numbers. The current phrasing mixes them. Should clarify that dome claims 5.3% but its own numbers give 10.8%.

WIN-029 detail_evidence Q >> 100 claim understates the physics. For pure copper at ~7 Hz, Q >> 1000 (Q>1000 for a cast-copper shell, hundreds for oxidized bronze) — orders of magnitude above observed Q ≈ 4-6. Current text says "Q >> 100" which is softer than warranted.

SEC-1.9 HTML hygiene: two missing

closing tags in the section prose. Before

What the critique does and does not claim and before

The shadow path is horizontal. Causes rendering issues.

The Aug 2026 eclipse track does not pass through polar regions — honest range for that track is roughly 1.10-1.33. Either drop the Aug 2026 scoping or use honest numbers.

These four are: WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058" — contradiction: after build-time expansion {{CA_DOME}}=2 but four WINs are listed. issues.description: Part 2b WIN-044 paragraph uses wrong FSF formula: "FSF(r) = exp(−r/λg)" — missing 1/r factor and BOU normalization. Correct formula is FSF(r) = (H(r)/r) / (H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) where H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619). Source: curmudgeon c3 cross-section consistency check.

issues.description: WIN-027 and WIN-057 list different "four fitted parameters" for V50.6 H(r). WIN-027 includes ellipse axis ratio but omits 14,105 km; WIN-057 includes 14,105 km but omits ellipse ratio. True union is at least five parameters. Pick one canonical list and use it in both WINs.

QF27/QF28 nonstop framing is fragile — Qantas has operated this corridor both nonstop and via Auckland. Underlying kill-shot (30,000 km disc route vs 11,338 km flight) holds either way; soften to "Direct Sydney-Santiago services" with reference to in-air time.

WIN-027 detail_evidence opens with circular-sounding "the correction exists because the Earth is curved, not because it is flat." Rephrase as "the correction exists because any sphere-to-plane projection produces exactly this distortion pattern — the dome rediscovered the curvature it denies."

detail_verdict_text appends the standardized post-hoc boilerplate paragraph that duplicates what detail_evidence already says with more specificity. Either drop the boilerplate or trim to one sentence with anchor-link to Part 3.1b.

WIN-027 geometric error: "Sydney sits on the ~20,015 km southern rim and Santiago on the opposing rim" is wrong — the flat disc has only one outer rim; both cities sit at r ≈ 16,000 km on the same southern arc. Fix: "On the flat disc, Sydney (~34°S) and Santiago (~33.5°S) sit near the southern edge at r ≈ 16,000 km from centre, separated by ~138° of azimuth; the planar chord between them exceeds ~30,000 km, almost three times the globe great-circle distance."

WIN-027 Karney/Vincenty sentence double-states the accuracy claim: parenthetical "(sub-millimetre absolute error on the full WGS84 ellipsoid, per Karney 2013 — accuracy does not degrade with distance)" restates the preceding "achieves sub-millimeter absolute accuracy on WGS84". Also inconsistent: American spelling "sub-millimeter" vs British standard. Consolidate to one clause with British spelling.

WIN-027 detail_verdict_text opens "21% unexplained error is substantial; spherical geodesy achieves <0.5% error" which clashes with the detail_evidence that now cites sub-millimetre absolute accuracy (essentially zero error). Tighten to: "R² 0.79 = 21% unexplained; spherical geodesy achieves sub-millimetre absolute accuracy globally with two parameters."

WIN-024 tldr_verdict factual error: "The SAA covers only a fraction of the latitude band." The whole argument is that the SAA covers only ~130° of LONGITUDE (not latitude). Change to: "The SAA covers only a fraction of the longitude band — about 130 degrees, while the winds encircle the entire Southern Hemisphere."

WIN-024 code_analysis.monitoring is "live_fetch" but current detail_evidence already quotes two hardcoded pass=True domains (Domain 27 and 33). Should be "hardcoded". This stale tag inflates the Part 2b live_fetch count and weakens the aggregated code-analysis finding.

WIN-024 tldr_evidence says "the SAA occupies only about 120° of longitude" but detail_evidence says 90°W–40°E = 130° (also stated in detail_verdict_text). Change tldr_evidence to 130° for consistency.

WIN-024 detail_evidence Domain 17 sentence does not explain WHY Domain 17 is permissive of independent atmospheric variation. Append: "— and the conditional design (AAO > 0.3 sigma WHEN SAA decay > 50 nT/yr) passes trivially in any month when the second clause is false, so the test is permissive of independent atmospheric variation."

WIN-068 detail_evidence P3 discusses E001-E009 2026 eclipse predictions. WIN-068 is about the historical 1991-2016 Kim & Chang ensemble, not future predictions. Move the E001-E009/FSF cascade content to Section 1.8 or predictions catalog for E001-E009.
issues.fix_note: WIN-068 detail_evidence no longer contains E001-E009 2026 eclipse predictions — content was removed in a prior patch cycle. Issue state was stale.

issues.description: detail_verdict_text says Kim & Chang data was available "well before" dome incorporated it, without a specific date. Specify V51.0 (early 2026) as dome-incorporation version, contrasting with 2018 paper publication — nine-year gap is stronger than "well before".

WIN-010 (BOU 2017) and WIN-025 (2024 9-station) share the same dome mechanism and verdict as WIN-068 but do NOT include refraction cascade arguments. If cascade applies to ensemble, it applies to all three equally; if not (per 2026-04-13 revert), WIN-068 cross-reference to Section 1.8 should be matched in WIN-010 and WIN-025 for consistency.

WIN-027 Scale comparison paragraph still says "an ellipse axis ratio" instead of r_eq = 14,105 km. The V50.6 paragraph was fixed in a prior cycle but the Scale comparison paragraph two paragraphs later still uses the old vague term. Creates internal self-contradiction within one WIN: one paragraph names r_eq correctly, the next calls it "ellipse axis ratio". Also the parameter list ("a base disc radius, an exponential height function, an ellipse axis ratio, and a longitude scale factor") mixes raw parameters and mathematical constructs inconsistently.

WIN-027 has three inconsistent precision claims: finding says "globe great-circle achieves <0.5% error"; tldr_evidence says "sub-meter precision globally"; detail_evidence says "sub-millimetre algorithmic accuracy" (Karney 2013). These describe different things and the inconsistency weakens the argument. The strongest honest claim is sub-millimetre algorithmic accuracy per Karney (2013). Fix: align finding and tldr_evidence to sub-millimetre.

Dome site reuses WIN-057 for two distinct claims: "Two-Zone Disc Topology" (our coverage, Prospective Weekly) and "SAA Simultaneous Acceleration" (Prospective list, uncovered by our review). The SAA Simultaneous Acceleration claim is a separate prospective claim that our wins.json does not evaluate. The dome number collision is itself evidence of numbering instability we critique, but the content divergence is an analytical coverage gap.
issues.notes: Analyst should decide: (a) add WIN-057b for SAA Simultaneous Acceleration (mirrors 058/058b pattern), or (b) add a note in WIN-057 prose about the numbering collision. Not urgent — the SAA claim is prospective with no current test window closure.
issues.resolution: WIN-057b added to wins.json via EXP-166 integration. SAA Global Cavity Re-Pressurization claim evaluated as Std Model Explains.

issues.description: WIN-068 finding text says "triple-counts the same phenomenon as WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068" — WIN-068 lists itself. Confusing and logically circular.
detail_verdict_text cites "Meza et al. 2021" without journal/volume/pages/DOI. Flagged c4 and c5 — still unresolved. If unverifiable, remove; Chapman 1933 alone is sufficient.
detail_verdict_text says "nine-year gap" but Kim & Chang published 2018, V51.0 early 2026 = 8 years. Off by one year. Should be "eight-year gap".
CASCADE cross-reference in detail_evidence says "seasonally indeterminate" — opaque for readers who haven't read Section 1.8. Recommend more self-contained phrasing that explains what the concern is.

Broken DOI: Alken et al. (2020), DOI 10.1029/2020GL087323 returns 404 from CrossRef. Citation supports claim that SAA second minimum near 20S 10E is a natural consequence of reverse-flux patch migration. Replacement: Alken et al. (2021, IGRF-13), DOI: 10.1186/s40623-021-01569-z, verified resolves.
issues.fix_details: Replaced broken DOI 10.1029/2020GL087323 with verified Alken et al. (2021) IGRF-13 DOI 10.1186/s40623-021-01569-z in WIN-057b detail_evidence

issues.description: WIN-068 detail_verdict_text P3: self-referential "This WIN also triple-counts" phrasing. A WIN cannot itself count — the dome author does the counting. Harmonize with sibling WINs (WIN-010, WIN-025) which use passive framing. Curmudgeon c6 hole 1.

WIN-068 detail_evidence P3: missing preemption of "both sides calibrate" defense (c5 advocate defense_survives=3, still not fully neutralized in c6). Sq models use independently measured ionospheric parameters (electron density, conductivity tensors from ionosondes/satellites), not from eclipse signal. Dome FSF calibrated circularly against the eclipse data it claims to predict. Curmudgeon c6 hole 2.

WIN-068 detail_evidence P2: cross-reference to Section 1.8 (refraction indeterminacy) creates tension with Std Model Explains verdict. A defender can argue: if physical sun altitude is irrelevant (verdict rationale), why cite its indeterminacy? Strengthen inoculation to clarify this is a dome-mechanism consistency concern only, not a challenge to the Chapman/Std-Model verdict. Curmudgeon c6 hole 3.

WIN-068 detail_verdict_text P4: "Under the dome author's own timestamping definition" lacks link to Timestamp Error tab. All other references to OTS methodology should cross-link to the dedicated write-up. Curmudgeon c6 hole 4.

WIN-015 detail_verdict_text contains FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW paragraph referencing legitimate-novelty carve-out. code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false and code_analysis.reviewed=true — Condition 1 of the carve-out is already known-false. Paragraph should be removed (carve-out not applicable). Also affects WIN-003 and WIN-011 which have identical FLAGGED paragraphs. ISS-1070 and ISS-1071 cover those.

WIN-015 detail_evidence cites Kuhlke (2010) from Journal of Scientific Exploration (JSE) — a fringe/anomalistics venue. Our review criticizes the dome for relying on non-peer-reviewed sources. Using JSE as corroboration undermines our source hygiene. Fix: remove Kuhlke sentence and fold content into Bruhn sentence attribution.

WIN-015 detail_evidence describes CUORE as "cryogenic neutrino detection at LNGS". CUORE (Cryogenic Underground Observatory for Rare Events) is a neutrinoless double-beta decay search, not a neutrino detection experiment. Fix: change to "CUORE (neutrinoless double-beta decay search at LNGS)" or "CUORE (cryogenic rare-event search at LNGS)".

WIN-015 tldr_evidence says "multiple physicists showed his math is internally invalid". The plural relies on Bruhn + Kuhlke. Once Kuhlke is removed (ISS-1065), the plural is unsupported. Fix: update to "a physicist at TU Darmstadt (Bruhn 2006) showed his mathematics is internally invalid, and the observed effect is explained by ordinary near-field electromagnetic coupling at short range — well-known antenna physics."

WIN-003 detail_verdict_text contains FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW paragraph referencing legitimate-novelty carve-out. code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false and code_analysis.reviewed=true — Condition 1 of the carve-out is already known-false. Same boilerplate as WIN-015 (ISS-1064) and WIN-011 (ISS-1071). Remove the FLAGGED paragraph.

WIN-011 detail_verdict_text contains FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW paragraph referencing legitimate-novelty carve-out. code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false and code_analysis.reviewed=true — Condition 1 of the carve-out is already known-false. Same boilerplate as WIN-015 (ISS-1064) and WIN-003 (ISS-1070). Remove the FLAGGED paragraph.

PRED-105 (Copper coils 46° +15% cucumber growth, 14-day window) test window closes 2026-04-18 (~1 day). Not yet in predictions.json. Analyst should add entry and monitor for dome-published result. Poller IMMINENT flag from 2026-04-17 summary.
issues.fix_details: PRED-105 entry added to data/predictions.json from analyst assessment PRED-assessment-PRED-105.json. Entry includes full detail_reasoning, tldr, tldr evidence of internal arithmetic failure (46° vs 25.25° from sin θ = H₀/R_eq), protocol gaps, and biology plausibility assessment. Verdict: pending (window closes 2026-04-18).

issues.description: WIN-015 defense_survives=3 in curmudgeon cycle 3 review. Best surviving defense: our refutation is theoretical (Bruhn attacks equations); Jackson §9.1 defines near-field zone but does not compute expected coupling magnitude for Meyl-class geometry. Need defense neutralization: add a quantitative back-of-envelope near-field coupling calculation (M = µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³)) to detail_evidence showing expected induced voltage at Meyl coil dimensions/frequencies.

WIN-015 detail_evidence argues near-field coupling is expected at Meyl coil geometry/frequencies, but never computes the expected coupling magnitude. Adding a representative M ≈ µ₀·(A·B)/(4πd³) calculation would shift burden of proof: we have quantified the near-field coupling, Meyl has not. See curmudgeon preemptive_recommendation in WIN-015.c3.json. Routed to analyst EXP-169.

Dome author added docs/api/current/methodology.json (commit 6e06efb, 2026-04-17) which adopts our OTS critique rhetorically — explicitly scoping "what_ots_proves" vs "what_ots_does_not_prove" in near-verbatim alignment with our Timestamp Error tab. This is a SOFT CANARY TRIGGER. The strict canary condition (separate predictions-only file with its own OTS anchor + per-claim temporal proof mechanism) is NOT yet met. Our Timestamp Error tab needs an update to address the new rhetorical position without conceding the underlying data-structure critique is resolved.
issues.fix_details: EXP-170 integrated: generate-html.js Timestamp Error tab update + two companion patches in sections.json (Section 1.4 TLDR, Part 8 Section 8.5 Directive #4). Canary remains at partial-rhetorical-adoption — promote to triggered only if a predictions-only file with its own OTS anchor is published.

issues.description: docs/api/current/claim_index.json now explicitly documents WIN number collisions in registry_integrity_notes — specifically WIN-058 (two different claims with same visible label) and WIN-062. These are WIN number collisions we have previously flagged. The dome's explicit acknowledgment in machine-readable form is useful: our review should cross-reference and confirm these entries match our own analysis of WIN-058 and WIN-062 inconsistencies.
issues.fix_details: Consistency check performed. Our wins.json tracks WIN-058 (058) and WIN-058b as separate entries — consistent with dome registry_integrity_notes collision. WIN-062 also tracked (Tesla 1.574c, retrospective_structural in dome claim_index.json). No prose change needed — our review already covers both collisions.

issues.description: WIN-032 detail_evidence contains hardcoded stale numbers: (1) "4 theological claims in the 67" — wins.json now has 71 WINs (not 67); (2) "95.2% accuracy figure" — dome_claimed_accuracy is 94.5% (not 95.2%). CLAUDE.md forbids hardcoded numbers that can be derived from data. Fix: use version-neutral phrasing "in the current WIN count" and "dome's claimed accuracy figure".
issues.fix_details: WIN-032 detail_evidence patched: removed stale version-specific numbers (39-domain→live monitoring, 67/95.2%→version-neutral). Commit 09a4086.

issues.description: WIN-032 detail_evidence says "does not include WIN-032 in its 39-domain audit engine" — 39-domain is V50.6 era. V51.0 live dashboard is a "20-Domain Convergence Dashboard" (raw-text/08-live-power.txt). Fix: version-neutral phrasing such as "live monitoring audit engine" or "monitoring audit engine".
issues.fix_details: WIN-032 detail_evidence patched: removed stale version-specific numbers (39-domain→live monitoring, 67/95.2%→version-neutral). Commit 09a4086.

issues.description: Prediction MT-003 (Schumann suppression G3 storm test) has is_genuinely_prospective=true but no tldr field. Every other genuinely-prospective prediction has one. Add a 2-3 sentence tldr to data/predictions.json MT-003 entry.
issues.fix_details: MT-003 already has tldr in predictions.json. Integrity report was from 09:00Z before tldr was added. No action needed.

issues.description: Dome author added docs/review-response.html (commit 5021eec "Harden site against strawman critiques", 2026-04-17) — most direct acknowledgement-of-critics signal to date. Our review should be aware of the arguments being deflected and should proactively address them. Analyst should read review-response.html and check whether any of our arguments are being mislabeled as strawmen, and whether the dome's rebuttals expose any genuine weaknesses.
issues.fix_details: EXP-171 analyzed review-response.html. All five "strawman" labels target positions our review does not hold or represent dome concessions that absorb our critique. No genuine weaknesses found. No prose changes needed.

issues.description: WIN-032 detail_verdict_text: second paragraph repeats the monitoring system exclusion point already made in first paragraph. Remove the redundant sentence ("The dome own monitoring system (monitor.py) contains no validation domain for this WIN") from the second paragraph.
WIN-032 detail_evidence: says "at least 4 theological claims in the current WIN count" without listing them. Enumerate or anchor-link: the four are WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034.

WIN-032 detail_evidence: phrase "doubly unfalsifiable" invites rebuttal that unfalsifiability is binary. Recast as "compounds two unfalsifiable layers: the dome geometry and the prophetic framework" to keep rhetorical force without implying degrees.

WIN-015 detail_verdict_text: drop "Kuhlke, 2010" from "(Bruhn, 2006; Kuhlke, 2010)" — JSE is fringe venue same class as Meyl uses. Change to "(Bruhn, 2006)" alone. Bruhn TU Darmstadt mathematical critique is self-supporting.

Timestamp Error tab ts-april-2026-update block: partial-sample extrapolation in "column under that header would still be empty" — uses classified sample not including WIN-035-039. Tighten to: "As of 2026-04-17, the prospective_confirmed column contains no confirmed WINs — only one pending prediction (pred-eclipse-tier3). The column could fill out as more of the 69 WINs get classified in claim_index.json; a test of the methodology good faith will be whether WIN-035–WIN-039 (the author own previously-declared prospective set) are tagged prospective_confirmed when classification is extended."

Timestamp Error tab ts-april-2026-update block: tension with ts-scope block — ts-scope block names WIN-035–WIN-039 as prospective set but taxonomy paragraph argues from sample not including them. Add sentence: "The dome author own earlier self-declared prospective set (WIN-035 through WIN-039) has not yet been classified in claim_index.json as of this writing; the partial sample is the author curated strongest_current_targets set, not his prospective subset."

Timestamp Error tab ts-april-2026-update block: force-push framing says "can be force-pushed without leaving a verifiable trace on the Bitcoin chain" but Section 1.4 detail body concedes git provides partial probabilistic integrity via server logs and public forks. Add: "can be force-pushed — detectable through GitHub server logs and public forks, but without a verifiable trace on the Bitcoin chain".

WIN-032 advocate_mode.defense_survives=3: dome defender can argue review applies unfalsifiability test asymmetrically (exempting standard physics). Need preemptive paragraph in detail_verdict_text: "The dome cannot use a weaker meaning of CONFIRMED for theological claims while keeping them in the same scorecard as claims with explicit falsification thresholds." See curmudgeon c3 review for full preemptive text.
issues.fix_details: EXP-174 analysis integrated: new paragraph in WIN-032 detail_verdict_text neutralizes asymmetric-unfalsifiability defense by showing mainstream physics separates theoretical consequences from empirical confirmations and does not mix them in the same scorecard.

issues.description: Prediction MT-003 (Schumann suppression G3 storm test) is is_genuinely_prospective=true but has no tldr field. Every other genuinely-prospective prediction has a tldr. Add 2-3 sentence tldr to MT-003 in predictions.json.
issues.fix_details: MT-003 already has a tldr field. Integrity report was based on old build state. False positive.

issues.description: SEC-2b.5: kappa tautology bullet incorrectly claims BOU 2017 eclipse was the same event used to fit kappa. In reality kappa numerator (-10.9 nT) is BOU 2017 but denominator (-6.5 uGal) is Mohe 1997 - two different events.
SEC-2b.5: "Borrowed from the standard model (5 domains)" header but 6 items listed.

SEC-2b.5: category overlap not disclosed in prose. Tesla Harmonic Series/Telluric Cutoff span STATIC+TAUTOLOGY; Equatorial Gravity spans BORROWED+TAUTOLOGY. EXP-172 JSON acknowledges overlap but rendered section dropped it.
SEC-2b.5: Aetheric Redshift Scale listed as BORROWED in the audit but also listed as "discriminating" in the summary paragraph - self-contradiction.
SEC-2b.5: "(domains 27 and 33)" opaque; should clarify as row indices in 39-row status_history.json snapshot.
SEC-2b.5: Selleri/Lorentz category description "not a data test at all" is a strawman against framework choice in principle. Should be tightened to the specific Sagnac/GPS-clock-offset observables.
SEC-2b.5: "pulsing green ring reading 100.0%" is rendering-dependent. Should describe as "prominent 100.0% score graphic".
SEC-2b.5: kappa value 1.67 used in TAUTOLOGY bullet but canonical formula_runtime.json value is 1.672. Should note dashboard rounding.
Part 4.1 (p4-41-0): stale 20-domain framing. Needs update for April 2026 dashboard expansion to 28 domains, Group B/C/D/E classification, cross-reference to SEC-2b.5.

SEC-2b.5: WIN labels in the domain audit table use the same WIN-NNN format as our review's WIN namespace, but these refer to the dome site's internal status_history.json source strings. Add "dome-WIN-" prefix or parenthetical at list start to disambiguate.
issues.fix_details: EXP-177 patches applied: P1 notation paragraph + P2 dome-prefix disambiguation in SEC-2b.5

issues.description: SEC-2b.5: H(r) parameters (8537, 8619) presented without caveat; dome author claims these are aetheric pressure/scale derivations from first principles rather than fitted constants. Section should soften "two numerical parameters" framing and link to Section 1.8 analysis showing post-hoc calibration.
issues.fix_details: Added first-principles caveat and Section 1.8 cross-reference to H(r) formula line in SEC-2b.5. Softened "two numerical parameters" to "two numerical constants".

issues.description: SEC-2b.5: dashboard-inflation ratio (28 domain labels / 10 independent knobs = 2.8x inflation factor) is a quotable one-liner left on the table. "At 28 scored domains for 10 independent numerical knobs, the dashboard has a 2.8x domain-inflation factor relative to its underlying parameter count." Travels well on social.
issues.fix_details: Added 2.8x domain-inflation factor sentence to SEC-2b.5 (28 domains / 10 independent knobs).

issues.description: WIN-032 broken cross-reference: detail_verdict_text says "our Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6)" but Pattern 6 in part9 is "Inconvenient Data Discarded" (not scorecard inflation). The anchor #posthoc-standard links to Part 3.1b correctly, but the "Pattern 6" label is wrong and will mislead readers. Fix: remove ", Pattern 6" or replace with correct cross-reference.

SEC-4.1 Group C count mismatch: body text and TLDR both say "5 import standard-model or globe-model values" but Group C table has 6 rows (K1, S2, M2 Tidal Periods + Equatorial Gravity + Aetheric Redshift + P-wave Shadow Zone). Fix: change both instances of "5" to "6".

SEC-2b.5 H(r) formula line says "Section 1.8 shows them as post-hoc calibrations against observational data" but Section 1.8 (Coordinate Collapse) demonstrates that 8537/8619 reproduce globe-model coordinate geometry; it does not explicitly discuss calibration origin or use the term "post-hoc calibrations". The cross-reference is imprecise. Fix: change to "Section 1.8 shows the same constants appearing in globe-model spherical geometry, consistent with fitting" or similar.

SEC-2b.5 H(r) line says "the dome author derives these as aetheric pressure-scale values from first principles" but raw-text search shows the dome site's "from first principles" claim is only a general homepage statement (01-home.txt), never applied specifically to 8537/8619. The dome source presents H(r) as an empirical V12 discovery (fitting three H values: ~9500, 4750, 9086 km), not a first-principles derivation. Fix: replace with "the dome characterizes these as fundamental aetheric scaling constants" or remove the specific attribution.

SEC-2b.5 formula list uses inconsistent terminology: H(r) uses "two numerical constants (8537, 8619)" but items 2-6 use "parameter(s)" ("one new parameter (0.20)", "one parameter (14105)", etc.). Fix: standardize all to "numerical constant(s)" to match the H(r) usage and to be more precise.

SEC-4.1 Group B table cites WIN-073, WIN-075, WIN-076 as "Prior WIN" column entries, but none of these WINs exist in wins.json. They are pending dome WINs not yet analyzed. Readers following cross-references find empty space. Either add placeholder WIN stubs or annotate these rows with "(pending analysis)".

WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 detail_evidence contains zero references to dome author's registry_integrity_notes disambiguation introduced in claim_index.json on 2026-04-17. Dome added claim_key slugs (win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-058-theta-rule, win-062-tesla-wave-speed) to machine-disambiguate the visible-prose WIN-number collision. Our prose still presents the collision as unacknowledged. A dome defender can cite the API disambiguation. Fix: add ~1 paragraph per WIN crediting the registry_integrity_notes as a documentary acknowledgement (April 17), noting visible-prose collision still unfixed in wins.html, and categorizing this as acknowledge-without-fix consistent with methodology.json ISS-1087 pattern. (Source: REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json curmudgeon meta-audit.)
WIN-053 attribution error: stated ~54,000 nT for pure centered dipole at geographic south pole; correct value is ~60,700 nT (IGRF-13 gives ~55,000 nT due to multipoles).

WIN-053 over-specific 1/r Griffiths claim: §5.3.3 applies to tightly wound solenoid; dome claims aetheric toroidal flow, not wound coil. Flux conservation (∇·B=0) is the stronger primary argument; Griffiths comparison should be secondary and bounded.
WIN-053 dome-side zero-field quote paraphrased not sourced: wins.html text for WIN-053 not captured in V50.6 dump; verbatim quote cannot be verified. Downgraded to characterization of dome argument.

SEC-2b.5 says the scored machinery runs on "roughly ten independent numerical knobs" but the actual count is 12: FORM-V51-001 (8537, 8619), FORM-V51-002 (0.20), FORM-V51-003 (14105), FORM-V51-004 (1.672), FORM-V51-005 (0.19550, 0.1640, 32.974), FORM-V51-006 (20015, 0.66), plus master constants (5733, 2534). 20015 appears in both FORM-V51-006 and as disc_radius master constant — counted once. Fix: change "ten" to "twelve" and update the 2.8x factor calculation (28/12=2.33x, not 2.8x).

SEC-2b.5 NMP Longitude is categorized under "Loose / directional (3 domains)" in the domain audit, but the body text says "Actually within 1% of observed. This is one of the few cells with real empirical content." The section then treats it as DISCRIMINATING in the summary paragraph. This is an internal contradiction: either it belongs in DISCRIMINATING (not LOOSE), or the LOOSE-section note must explain why it stays there despite tight fit. Current tally of "3 LOOSE + 1 DISCRIMINATING = 28" is ambiguous on whether NMP Longitude is double-counted.

SEC-2b.5 BORROWED count arithmetic mismatch: the tally says "6 BORROWED" and the "Borrowed from the standard model" subsection lists 6 items (K1, S2, M2, Equatorial Gravity, Aetheric Redshift, P-wave Shadow Zone). However, the domain-by-domain audit also labels Schumann Frequency (in the Tesla-family section) as BORROWED. If Schumann is BORROWED, total BORROWED = 7, and the tally (14+7+2+3+2+1=29≠28) is wrong. Either Schumann must be reclassified or Equatorial Gravity (which is both BORROWED and TAUTOLOGY per the overlap note) must be moved to TAUTOLOGY-only to keep total at 28.

SEC-4.1 Bottom Line paragraph says "Of the remaining cells, 6 import standard-model or globe-model values, 2 are Selleri / Lorentz framework recastings, and 3 are loose directional checks" but Group A (14) + Group B (14) = 28 with no remaining cells. Groups C, D, E overlap with A/B rather than being additional cells. The Bottom Line language implies extra cells beyond 28, which is wrong. Fix: change "Of the remaining cells" to language acknowledging Groups C/D/E are overlapping cross-cutting classifications within the 28.

SEC-4.1 Bottom Line says "The dome model's λg = 8,619 km is the ruler" but Group A row 2 (Tesla Longitudinal Freq) lists shared constants as "λg, va" — va is an independent fitted parameter not derivable from λg. The one-ruler framing is therefore incomplete; there are at least two distinct fitted constants driving Group A domains. Fix: update the ruler sentence to acknowledge both λg and va (and H₀).

SEC-2b.5 H(r) description says "Section 1.8 shows they reproduce globe-model coordinate geometry, consistent with fitting to observational data rather than derivation from first principles." Section 1.8 (Coordinate Collapse) does not literally show H(r) fitting — it shows coordinate system dependence on globe geometry. The stronger and more accurate citation is the dome author's own V12 model log: H(r) "reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements" (Schumann ~9,500 km, Polaris 4,750 km, model 9,086 km) — a two-parameter fit through 3 points is definitionally post-hoc calibration. Fix: cite the V12 model log directly instead of claiming Section 1.8 shows this.
issues.fix_details: ISS-1121 was about H(r) citation in SEC-2b.5. EXP-182 P1 replaced 2b.5 with a pointer-stub that contains no H(r) content. Issue is moot — problematic text was removed.

issues.description: SEC-4.1 does not disclose that Equatorial Gravity is double-classified (Group A lambda_g-dependent AND Group C BORROWED/standard-model). A defender could cite this undisclosed double-count as evidence of our table being misleading. Fix: add a note in the Group C table row for Equatorial Gravity acknowledging it also appears in Group A (double-classified), or in the Note on category overlap at the Bottom Line.
issues.fix_details: EXP-182 P3 new 4.1 content explicitly discloses: Equatorial Gravity is both BORROWED and TAUTOLOGY, counted once BORROWED. Exactly as ISS-1120 required.

issues.description: WIN-019: WRONG arXiv identifier — "arXiv:1302.7179" is a condensed-matter physics paper (Da Pieve et al., Cr-doped TiO2 magnetism), NOT an analemma paper. Correct: arXiv:1302.0765 (Holbrow, "Build your own analemma"). Also: Holbrow is single-author, not "et al."

WIN-019: WRONG Lynch Bulletin volume — "Irish Math. Soc. Bulletin 70" is wrong. The analemma paper is Bulletin 69 (Summer 2012, pp. 47-56). Bulletin 70 contains a Mirimanoff polynomials paper, no analemma content.

WIN-019: Perimeter ratio omitted from list. Independent computation gives lobe perimeter ratio ≈ 2.56 — still differs from dome claimed 2.66 (at 2-sig-fig precision). Add to the list to close the loophole.

WIN-020: detail_verdict_text incorrectly applies the "hardcoded expected value in same commit as live fetch" language, but WIN-020 has code_analysis.monitoring="none" — there is no fetch and no hardcoded value. The period appears only as a metadata label. The verdict paragraph is internally inconsistent.

WIN-058: detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph says "The numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters" — but detail_evidence explicitly says θ = −lonE is NOT derived from dome geometry; it is a coordinate convention. Self-contradiction.

WIN-058: finding says "V13 merely dropped V12's unnecessary solar-noon-UTC indirection" — wrong on two counts: (1) solar-noon-UTC proxy was V9-era, not V12 (V12 introduced 0.9941 scaling); (2) V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor is still live in distance_analysis.py, so nothing was actually dropped.
WIN-058: "183° error" for Sydney uses linear arithmetic; correct minimum angular distance is ≈176° (min(183.7°, 360−183.7°) = 176.3°). The qualitative point is unchanged but the number is wrong.

SEC-4.1 (part4): Bottom Line parenthetical (lambda_A excluded because substitutes H0) is redundant with lambda_A BORROWED row-10 classification. Reads as legacy text. Fix: delete parenthetical. Source: SEC-4.1-post-EXP-182.c1 HOLE-2.

issues.description: part8b uses Kill-Shot #1 label 3x to refer to Section 4.1 (the 47.69-sigma independence audit). The actual Kill-Shot #1 in Part 5 is the Sydney-Perth distance test. Trivially exploitable by dome defenders.

part8b says Part 4.2 Registry Framing Audit is "pending integration via EXP-183" in 2 places. EXP-183 is already integrated as of 2026-04-18. Also: Part 4 now has two subsections numbered 4.2 (Eclipse + Registry Framing Audit) — a display collision part8b should disambiguate.

part8b L3 eclipse layer bundles PRED-095 (magnetic, -17 to -21 nT threshold) with PRED-TIER-2 (gravity, recycled WIN-011). The magnetic threshold is incorrectly applied as a combined criterion. Layers must be separated.
part8b 8b.4 TLDR says WIN-058 verdict reflects "pending" but WIN-058 actual verdict is Misleading. Body paragraph correctly says Misleading. TLDR inconsistency.

part8b 8b.6 uses "multi-file push window" but names 3 commits (6e06efb, 5021eec, a57e731) that may span multiple git pushes. "Detection window" or "24-hour window" is the correct framing for our monitoring frame.

EXP-183 proposes new Part 4.2 "Registry Framing Audit" section but SEC-4.2 already exists (August 2026 Eclipse). Numbering conflict. Resolution: either insert as 4.6 (after 4.5 Infographic), or renumber existing 4.2-4.5 as 4.3-4.6. Replacement HTML is in EXP-183 (6888 chars) and is ready to integrate once numbered correctly. Needs human decision on placement.
issues.fix_details: Resolved: renumbered Registry Framing Audit from 4.2 to 4.6 (Part 4 already had a 4.2 Eclipse section). Updated 3 cross-references in part8b.

issues.description: Commit attribution inconsistent across three WIN blocks (EXP-181 integration). WIN-058 says "in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17)", WIN-058b says "in a single 24-hour window", WIN-062 says "inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17". Defenders can ask: one commit or 24-hour window? Standardize to "in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)" across all three. Note: WIN-058 already updated to "in commit 6e06efb" by current run patch. WIN-058b and WIN-062 need update. Source: WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 curmudgeon review HOLE-5.

WIN-058 and WIN-058b: onclick="showTab('timestamp');return false" for the Timestamp Error tab anchor drops the default anchor jump to id="timestamp-error". Reader arrives at top of tab rather than specific April 2026 update block. Not a broken link but worse-than-possible UX. Lowest priority — cosmetic. Verify anchor jump behavior in switched-tab DOM before fixing. Source: WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 curmudgeon review HOLE-6.

SEC-4.1 (part4): Row 22 (Mascon Gravity Anomaly) classified STATIC with inline self-acknowledgment "STATIC out of model scope". Curmudgeon notes this could be moved to LOOSE since it is a prediction domain that happens to fall outside current formula parameters. If moved: non-overlap tally updates from 12 STATIC + 7 MIXED + 1 BORROWED to 13 STATIC + 6 MIXED + 1 BORROWED (net neutral on headline). Moving it acknowledges the model could in principle generate mascon predictions — a small concession with low risk. Source: SEC-4.1-post-EXP-182.c1 HOLE-5.

SEC-4.1 (part4): Bookkeeping footnote on 67 CONFIRMED / 69 confirmed mismatch (live.html tombstone vs daily-review text) says "we note it here for completeness but do not build further argument on it". Curmudgeon recommends noting this discrepancy is relevant to ISS-1132 (three-layer 10/9/1 count drift). The 67 vs 69 adds a FOURTH layer to the count disagreement argument. Consider strengthening: replace "we note it here for completeness" with "this adds a fourth discrepancy to the count-layer pattern (see also the 10/9/1 prospective_confirmed drift)". Source: SEC-4.1-post-EXP-182.c1 HOLE-6.

A defender can say "your own monitor will auto-mark this as self-healing even if the author never manually fixes it — proving the audit captures noise." issues.title: WIN-053: boilerplate monitor.py verdict text
issues.description: H1 (major) from WIN-053.c4: detail_verdict_text second paragraph says "hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call" — WIN-053 has no monitor.py domain at all; text is boilerplate from other WINs.

issues.title: WIN-053: IGRF-13 coefficient count wrong (168 vs 195)
issues.description: H3 (minor) from WIN-053.c4: detail_evidence says "168 spherical harmonic coefficients" for IGRF-13. IGRF-13 main field is degree n=13 giving Σ(2n+1) for n=1..13 = 195 Gauss coefficients. 168 = degree-12 count.

issues.title: WIN-053: Sub-H(r) formula δ=6371km in detail_extra lacks dome source
issues.description: H4 (minor) from WIN-053.c4: detail_extra references Sub-H(r)=H(r)×(1−e^(−r/δ)) with δ=6,371 km but provides no URL/anchor to the V51 dome page. V50.6 raw-text archive does not contain this formula. Need verbatim dome quote + URL.

issues.title: WIN-062: Source attribution error — Tesla 1905 patent vs 1899 Colorado Springs Notes
issues.description: H1 (major) from WIN-062.c4: detail_verdict_text says "1.574c figure was published by Tesla in 1905 (US Patent 787412) and restated in his Colorado Springs Notes (Nikola Tesla Museum, Beograd 1978)." Two errors: (a) dome's own attribution is to 1899 Colorado Springs Notes, not the 1905 patent; (b) Tesla died in 1943 and cannot have "restated" anything in 1978 — the notes were published POSTHUMOUSLY. Fix: attribute to 1899 Colorado Springs experiments, note 1905 patent depicts spherical Earth, and 1978 = posthumous publication.

issues.title: WIN-062: "verbatim" imprecision — dome re-expresses in disc-diameter units
issues.description: H2 (moderate) from WIN-062.c4: "adopts this published number verbatim" is imprecise. Tesla's 471,240 km/s converts to 1.5719c, not 1.574c. The dome produces 1.574c via v·t=d with disc_diameter=40,030 km, re-expressing the value in dome geometry. "Verbatim" gives a defender an opening.

issues.title: WIN-062: "century-old patent value" — same source error as H1 in condensed form
issues.description: H3 (moderate) from WIN-062.c4: final sentence says "a century-old patent value does not qualify" — if 1.574c is NOT a patent value (it comes from 1899 Notes), calling it "patent value" fails. Change to "century-old published velocity" or "127-year-old published figure".

issues.title: SEC-8b.3: Fail-clause wording too absolute ("cannot be triggered")
issues.description: H6 (minor) from SEC-8b.3.c2: L3a body says "the Fail-clause cannot be triggered without abandoning the Pass-clause's framing" — this is the strong "logically impossible" claim that EXP-191 conceded would be softened. Replace with softer wording: "triggering the Fail-clause would require abandoning the Pass-clause's framing."

issues.title: SEC-8b.3: "Registered 2026-04-04" is our tracker date not dome's
issues.description: H8 (minor) from SEC-8b.3.c2: text says "Genuinely prospective (registered 2026-04-04...)" — this is our predictions.json tracker registration date. Per raw-text/03-predictions.txt the dome OpenTimestamps-anchored on 2026-03-12, three weeks earlier. Fix to distinguish dome date from our tracker date.

Initially ran Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile; current daily_review.json shows provider=local, model=deterministic-summary (pipeline replaced). Monitor daily_review.json for dome-authored AI commentary that may mischaracterize our review or produce AI-generated rebuttals. Poller should track daily_review.json for changes. Note: SEC-8.17 section updated to reflect Groq→local transition.

WIN-058b.c4: Two onclick="showTab(...)" anchors in WIN-058b (WIN-012 reference and Timestamp Error reference) have return false, cancelling the default anchor jump. A reader clicking "WIN-012" lands at top of Wins tab, not at the WIN-012 panel. Fix: remove "return false" from both onclick handlers so the default anchor jump runs after the tab switch.

issues.title: SEC-8b.3: TLDR Chapman attribution imprecise — -17 to -21 nT not a first-principles prediction
issues.description: H5 (moderate) from SEC-8b.3.c2: TLDR says "Chapman's Sq-dynamo mechanism predicts the same -17 to -21 nT range" but Chapman (1933) gave a QUALITATIVE mechanism; the specific range emerges only from curve-fitting 39-eclipse ensemble. Attribution overstates Chapman's precision. Revise TLDR second sentence.

issues.title: SEC-8b.3: PRED-TIER-2 referenced without definition or cross-link
issues.description: H7 (minor) from SEC-8b.3.c2: L3b label "(L3b, PRED-TIER-2, gravity)" references PRED-TIER-2 without defining it in-section. Reader of 8b.3 in isolation will not know what it is. Add parenthetical or cross-link.

issues.title: WIN-062: three-condition Part 3.1b carve-out only cites condition (2)
issues.description: H4 (minor) from WIN-062.c4: verdict_text quotes Part 3.1b condition (2) (not plausibly known) but carve-out has THREE conjunctive conditions. Optionally append that derivation (condition 1) is also not unique to the dome.

issues.title: WIN-053: North pole field strength overstated (58-60k→54-56k nT)
issues.description: H5 (minor) from WIN-053.c4: detail_evidence says "~58,000–60,000 nT at the north magnetic pole" but WMM2025/IGRF-13 value is ~54,000–56,000 nT. South pole (~66 kT) is approximately correct.

WIN-020 c4 (carryover from c2/c3): detail_evidence may still contain a Saros/nodal cycle conflation. Check detail_evidence for any passage that conflates the 18.6-year nodal cycle with the 18-year Saros cycle; the Saros is ~18 years (223 synodic months ≈ 6585 days) which is close but distinct from the 18.6-year nodal cycle. If conflation exists, patch the distinction.

WIN-020 c4: Paragraph-frame discontinuity. Paragraph 2 attacks WIN-020 on post-hoc/trivial-transcription grounds; paragraph 3 attacks on mechanism grounds. These are two independent attacks presented in sequence without a signpost paragraph explaining they are cumulative, not alternatives. A reader can parse them as contradictory ("the claim is trivial" vs "the claim is scientifically wrong"). Consider adding a one-sentence bridge: "Both critiques apply independently."

EXP-183 includes a 2-3 sentence timestamp_tab_callout_html (methodology.json 5-class claim taxonomy cross-link) to insert near methodology references in the Timestamp Error tab. Infrastructure change (generate-html.js ~line 1520-1640). Content ready in EXP-183.timestamp_tab_callout_html.

WIN-001 c3 H0: detail_extra contains three back-to-back copies of the selective-damping-signal paragraph ("The selective damping signal. If the 26% aetheric damping factor from WIN-002 is applied consistently..."). Triplicated text renders as three identical stacked paragraphs on published site, trivial target for dome defenders. Fix: keep exactly one copy; remove internal restatement within that copy. Target detail_extra ~700-900 chars.

WIN-001 c3 H3: r_disc=πR_earth identity not explicitly stated in detail_evidence. Current text mentions va≈πc/2 but never closes the loop: r_disc=20,015 km = π×6371 = 20015.09 km to 4 decimal places. This is the strongest single forensic datapoint that r_disc is Earth half-circumference relabeled, not a dome-derived parameter. Add one explicit sentence.

WIN-001 c3 H4: detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph uses generic boilerplate listing IGRF/NASA station archives/ocean-tide gauges/telluric records as data sources. For WIN-001 the confirming value comes from Tesla Colorado Springs Notes 1899 and US Patent 787412 (1905) — none of the enumerated datasets apply. Replace generic list with actual WIN-001 source.

WIN-001 c3 H5: "On a flat disc, diametrically opposite from an off-center point has no clean geometric meaning" framing gives defender a rhetorical beat before the 14,300 km figure lands. Soften to specify the natural disc interpretation (reflection through pole → Antarctic rim at ~14,300 km, wrong by 12%; full disc diameter 40,030 km, wrong by 3×) rather than claiming no meaning exists.

WIN-011 citation misattribution: quote "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" is attributed to Wang et al. (2000, Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101) via "The paper explicitly states", but this quote appears in Yang & Wang (2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245, DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985), not the 2000 paper. The 2000 paper claims a shielding-consistent signal; the 2002 reanalysis walks it back. Fetching the 2000 paper finds no such quote — creates false-citation vulnerability. WIN-012 already correctly attributes this quote to Yang & Wang 2002. Fix: re-attribute in WIN-011 and add explicit 2002 citation.

WIN-053 c6 H0: tldr_evidence overstates coefficient-to-observation match. "Both coefficients match the observed WMM pole strengths precisely because they were fitted to them" — A=62,376 nT vs observed ~54,000-56,000 nT at NMP is ~11-15% gap; C=64,852 nT vs ~65,000-67,000 nT at SMP is ~3%. "Precisely" is misleading for the north pole coefficient. Revise to note the south-pole match is close but north-pole match is approximate.

WIN-053 c6 H1: TLDR cross-reference format inconsistency. tldr_verdict contains plain-text "(see §6.1)" while detail_evidence (c5 patch) correctly upgraded the Section-6.4 reference to a clickable HTML anchor. Readers who stay collapsed will see an unclickable section reference while the expanded text has a working link. Update tldr_verdict §6.1 reference to use the same clickable anchor pattern.

WIN-037: Sentence triplication bug in detail_evidence — the WIN-042 cross-reference sentence appears THREE times in a row at the end. Patch-application defect introduced by prior self-apply. Easy fix: collapse to single instance.

WIN-037: Near-duplicate Domain 8/Tsumeb sentence in detail_evidence. Two sentences in the same paragraph both state Domain 8 checks only Tsumeb. First is weaker ("never truly global"); second is more specific ("single INTERMAGNET station (Tsumeb) in the SAA core"). Remove the first; keep the second.

WIN-037: Code-analysis misattribution. detail_evidence says Domain 8 checks Tsumeb against a 28 nT threshold. But Domain 8 actually shows 77.0 nT/yr PRED=OBS identity (20% tolerance). WIN-037's 28 nT claim is a static W011 confirmation, not a live Domain 8 check. Rewrite to distinguish static W011 entry from Domain 8 live panel.

WIN-037: Unused sub-finding: dome confirmation uses 14-month window (Jan 2025–Mar 2026) not 12 months. Annualized: ~30 nT / 14 months × 12 = 25.7 nT/yr — BELOW the dome's own 28 nT/yr threshold. Add one sentence kicker to detail_evidence.

WIN-037: Rate vs cumulative wording ambiguity. The summary claim says "≥28 nT" (cumulative) but the model parameter is "≥28 nT/year" (rate). These are only equivalent over 12 months. Add one sentence making the WIN-037/WIN-042 duplication airtight via this equivalence.

DOI link https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11 in detail_evidence returns 404. The underlying Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 book exists but the chapter DOI is broken. Fix: replace with ISBN-based reference or Springer book URL.

HeartMath GCI link https://www.heartmath.org/gci-research/ in detail_verdict_text returns 404. Replace with Sentman 1987 (doi:10.1029/RS022i004p00595) or drop and keep only Nickolaenko & Hayakawa ref.

detail_verdict_text says Schumann fundamental "was published by Schumann in 1952" — factually wrong. Schumann 1952 is a theoretical prediction; first measurement was König & Schumann 1954. Minor but attackable.

code_analysis.monitoring="none" is stale. 08-live-power.txt now has Domain: P-wave Shadow Zone panel with Predicted/Observed/Tolerance/Status. Tag should be "hardcoded" (Tolerance: N/A, static depth-mismatched Observed string).
The "11,600-15,600 km surface distance" parenthetical in detail_evidence uses sphere math inside flat-disc rebuttal without flagging it. Add clarifier: "(using sphere math — which is the point)".

Near-duplicate phrase in detail_evidence: two consecutive sentences both end with "batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date." Looks sloppy; collapse into one sentence.

detail_evidence uses "12-14 months" which could be read as us conceding the window mismatch we just established. Rephrase to "dome's 14-month reporting window" to attribute the mismatch to dome, not to us.

WIN-012.c4: Direct quote "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" attributed to dome raw-text source notes is NOT present on the live dome site (WebFetch 2026-04-20 of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html). Requires live-site verification or reframing as historical/paraphrased with capture date.

issues.description: WIN-038 tldr_evidence: "~100 km ionospheric height on a spherical Earth" is incorrect as the causal explanation for 7.83 Hz. The Schumann fundamental is set by Earth's circumference (~40,075 km), not the ionospheric ceiling height. The 100 km height governs cavity losses/damping (lowering the fundamental from ~10.6 Hz lossless to ~7.83 Hz) but is not the primary resonance determinant. A dome defender using quarter-wave on 100 km gets 749 Hz and can call our TLDR physics-illiterate.
issues.fix_details: Updated tldr_evidence to correctly explain Schumann frequency via circumference, not ionospheric height

issues.description: SEC-5.0.c1: Framing asymmetry — the phrase "The rule was strengthened for the author and relaxed for the test — which is the opposite of what a falsification rule is supposed to do" is a rhetorical move that conflates two distinct critiques: (1) the new rule is epistemically more rigorous (credit the author), and (2) but as applied to THIS page it converts it from a falsification instrument into a methodology-of-good-evidence exhibit. Needs to be rewritten to separate these two points clearly. Assigned analyst for prose rewrite.

issues.description: defense_survives=3 in WIN-038.c3 review. Best defense: ECM could claim quarter-wave formula does not apply at Earth-scale, only to small resonators. Requires neutralization prose.

WIN-067: STALE TERMINOLOGY — "toroidal sump node" not found in V51.2 dome text. detail_claim, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_verdict_text all attribute this exact phrase to the dome, but current raw-text only has "Sub-terrestrial structure" in 08-live-power.txt. WIN-067: Quantitative error in refraction-cascade paragraph. Text says "at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 17,000–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–800 km" but H(17,000) = 8,537·exp(−17,000/8,619) = 1,188 km, not 1,400. Fix: use "r ≈ 15,500–20,015 km, H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km" or "r ≈ 17,000–20,015 km, H(r) ≈ 1,190–840 km".

WIN-067: Vague closing claim in refraction-cascade paragraph: "gravitational-index coupling effects catastrophically inconsistent with GRACE satellite gravimetry" has no mechanism or numbers. Either drop the closing sentence or replace with specific mechanism: n ≈ 1.2–2.8 bends light by degrees (see Section 1.8); whether same medium influences inertial acceleration at same scale is model-dependent and unspecified.

WIN-067: Generic post-hoc paragraph in detail_verdict_text uses boilerplate source list (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records) inappropriate for a gravity WIN. Replace generic list with gravity-specific sources: GRACE/GRACE-FO Level-2 products (JPL archive) and Bouguer-anomaly compilations (Pappa et al. 2019, Forte 2010, Ferraccioli et al. 2011).

WIN-053: Internal inconsistency on southern comparator. EXP-194 paragraph 2 pins dome B(r) validation to "~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole" but paragraph 3 references "~65,000–67,000 nT at the South Magnetic Pole per WMM2025". Use geographic pole throughout for paragraph 3 (preferred) or update paragraph 2 to use magnetic pole. Mixing geographic and magnetic pole framing is internally inconsistent.

WIN-053: Overclaim — "explicitly INTERMAGNET / CHAOS-7" attributes a specific source specification to the dome that isn't verbatim. Change "explicitly the INTERMAGNET ground-station network and the CHAOS-7 satellite-and-observatory composite" to softer phrasing: "the surface-observatory sources the dome's own home page highlights (INTERMAGNET ground stations and CHAOS-7)".

WIN-068: finding field says "Dome adds no equation" — technically inaccurate because dome publishes delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF. More precise phrasing (matching WIN-010/025): "Dome adds no dome-derived equation — its FSF scaling formula is empirically calibrated per-station, not derived from disc-radius geometry."

defense_survives=3 in WIN-064.c3 review. Best defense: dome claims disc cosmology is not incompatible with shadow zone (non-contradiction, not prediction), and radially symmetric sub-terrestrial structure preserves azimuthal symmetry around disc center. Requires neutralization prose.

WIN-012.c5 H2 (major): Dome has reframed C1/C4 gravity signals as a POSITIVE prediction via dielectric boundary transition model. Live WIN-011 (verified 2026-04-21) cites this mechanism. Our text does not engage the current dome position — it argues against an abandoned position. Need new paragraph explaining dielectric boundary model creates 3 new problems: (i) PRED-TIER-2 -10 to -13 uGal is 50-100% larger than Mohe -6.5 uGal; (ii) ECM pipeline filters out exactly the signal this model predicts (SG-null rescue self-sabotage); (iii) kappa was derived under 2000 shielding interpretation now abandoned, re-using same value is parameter adoption not prediction.

WIN-038 EXP-198 paragraph: closing sentence "no configuration" overclaims — cylindrical-waveguide Bessel-mode disc-cavity modes not addressed. TM_01 gives ~5.7 Hz, TE_11 gives ~4.4 Hz — neither matches 7.83 Hz from dome geometry, but claim "no configuration" is not demonstrated until Bessel-mode route is explicitly closed. Recommend: add one sentence closing this route, or narrow closing to "no mode formula the dome has published or could import from globe physics."

issues.description: SEC-8b.3: Category-error citation. Section invokes WIN-068 derives_from_dome:false tag to dismiss PRED-095's derivation path, but WIN-068 and PRED-095 are different targets. WIN-068 is about eclipse magnetometer — its code_analysis tag says the dome does not derive the prediction from dome geometry; it says nothing about PRED-095's physics. Needs fix: cite PRED-095 directly, or drop the WIN-068 tag invocation and make the argument from PRED-095's own content.

SEC-8b.3: Dual framing of −17 to −21 nT is incoherent. Section simultaneously treats the band as (a) something we publish under adoption and (b) the dome's effective commitment. A sophisticated reader will ask: "Is this our claim or their claim?" These are distinct epistemic positions that need clean separation: which parts of the -17 to -21 nT specification are we attributing to the dome, and which are we supplying as the adoption threshold?

SEC-8b.3: Load-bearing unsupported claim — TLDR and body both assert "calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations predict the same −17 to −21 nT range." No citation for this claim. If true this is the heart of the non-discrimination argument; if unsupported it is the most vulnerable claim in the section. Needs a primary citation (e.g., Malin & Chapman 1970 or equivalent) or the claim must be weakened.

SEC-8b.3: Anticipated-objection "monitor.py is internal tooling, not a public commitment; the predictions page is the forward commitment" is documented in EXP-195 anticipated_objections but NOT engaged in the published section text. A reader who encounters the monitor.py −29 nT presentation without the response will be confused about why we are citing internal code. This objection deserves to be surfaced and closed in-line.

SEC-8b.3: "internal code adds multipliers (KAPPA, latitude factor) producing a third value near −29 nT" lumps two distinct internal formulas. Per WIN-025 globe-fingerprint, monitor.py produces −28.94 nT (BOU baseline × KAPPA) and a second formula using the latitude factor produces a different value. Needs clean separation of which multiplier produces which intermediate result.

SEC-8b.3: "also predict" is loose wording. "Calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits... predict the same −17 to −21 nT range" + "also" suggests independent agreement; the actual claim is structural (same baseline × coverage × weighting arithmetic yields overlapping range, not independent confirmation). Recommend: "are consistent with" or "produce overlapping predictions for" instead of "also predict."

WIN-067 detail_evidence: V51.1 version citation updated to V51.2 (self-applied). Remaining moderate holes: (a) 9.826 m/s² figure in detail_extra needs primary-source citation (currently "advocate's own cited value" attribution removed but citation not yet added); (b) Domain-4-fails-own-rule argument at r=20,015 km (evaluates to 9.7854 m/s², violates ">0.1% vs WGS84" by 4.8×) not yet added to detail_extra — identified as strongest available argument by c5; (c) Net-effect paragraph needs rescope-retreat closing sentence. Batching as one moderate for next analyst follow-up.

WIN-012 detail_evidence (post-EXP-201): Three moderate tightenings from c6 review: (a) "at comparable latitude and instrument class" unsupported by PRED-TIER-2 spec — PRED-TIER-2 names no station/latitude/instrument class; (b) kappa-arithmetic argument needs to anticipate dome's independent-dielectric-derivation escape (if dome produces genuine dielectric derivation of PRED-TIER-2 not passing through kappa, internal contradiction dissolves); (c) self-filtering-detector dichotomy bypassed by frequency-specific filter escape — needs filter-spec demand. All three are tightenings to an otherwise sound paragraph, not retractions.

WIN-067: headline number mismatch. finding/tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict/detail_verdict_text all cite 170× but detail_extra (EXP-203 block) establishes 1,430× as the apples-to-apples Antarctic-latitude figure. Reader sees 170× four times then 1,430× in closing paragraph without reconciliation. SELF-APPLIED partial fix: added clarifying note in detail_extra that 1,430× strengthens rather than replaces the 170× headline. Full fix (updating TLDRs and finding to lead with 1,430×) deferred to analyst follow-up EXP.

issues.description: WIN-067 detail_extra paragraph (ii): unit conversion error. Text reads "-40 to -60 mGal ≈ 5×10^-5 m/s²" but correct conversion is 1 mGal = 10^-5 m/s², so 50 mGal = 5×10^-4 m/s². Downstream claim "widens the miss by another two orders of magnitude" should be "one order of magnitude" (to ~17,700×). A dome defender can disprove this with a two-line unit check. SELF-APPLIED.
issues.fix_details: Decider self-applied: corrected 5×10^-5 → 5×10^-4 m/s² and "two orders" → "one order of magnitude (to ~17,700×)". Build + 3403 tests pass.

issues.description: WIN-014 detail_evidence "would have captured" claim cites PRED-TIER-3 Level-3 residual methodology match as its basis. Curmudgeon c3 hole 2: this assumes 2009 Chinese SG methodology matches PRED-TIER-3 processing, but PRED-TIER-3 specifies Level-3 residuals specifically. The argument is stronger if it cites the SG noise floor independently: superconducting gravimeters have raw noise floors <1 µGal at minute timescales (Van Camp 2005; Crossley et al. 2013), so a 10–13 µGal pulse would appear even without Level-3 processing.
issues.fix_details: Decider self-applied 2026-04-22 in patch batch. Build + 3403 tests pass.

issues.description: WIN-014 detail_evidence cites "Sun et al., 2010" for China SG null result. Curmudgeon c3 hole 3: closest English-language candidate is a 2013 Chinese Journal of Geophysics paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) — a 3-year date mismatch. Current text acknowledges this uncertainty but attributes it to "Sun et al., 2010" without flagging the unverified date. Should qualify: "(Sun et al., attributed 2010; closest verified English-language record is doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, published 2013)" or resolve the discrepancy.
issues.fix_details: Decider self-applied 2026-04-22 in patch batch. Build + 3403 tests pass.

issues.description: WIN-014 detail_evidence re-asserts ~1,000× SG-vs-spring sensitivity from WIN-013 context (Van Camp et al. 2001, European SG vs Mohe spring). Soften to: "the dome's own WIN-011 positive-signal claim is based on Mohe spring-gravimeter data; if a −6.5 µGal coupling-mediated signal exists, both instrument classes should see it at their respective noise floors."

WIN-038 detail_evidence argument (iv) closes the flat-top cylindrical waveguide Bessel modes but does not address the H(r)-specific coupled eigenvalue escape. A physics-sophisticated defender will argue the honest calculation is a coupled radial-vertical eigenvalue problem on the dome's specific H(r). The counter: even a dome-specific H(r) Bessel problem must match 7.83 Hz — numeric eigenvalue solvers would need to find 7.83 as the fundamental with H(0)=8,537 km, which constrains the model. Needs a brief parenthetical addition. Analyst EXP needed.

WIN-067 detail_extra: '~9.826 m/s² Antarctic-interior gravity' is mislabeled. 9.826 m/s² corresponds to ~70°S (Antarctic coast), not interior (80-90°S where values are 9.8306-9.8322). Options: (a) drop 'interior' and specify 'Antarctic-latitude (~70°S)' — making explicit this is a conservative choice; or (b) re-anchor to true interior (9.8306 at 80°S) for a ~5,600× ratio. Option (a) preferred for rhetorical safety. Source: WIN-067.c6 hole 2.

WIN-067 finding does not disclose that the 1,430× ratio assumes linear g∝H(r) coupling. Under H^(1/5) (most forgiving monotonic scaling) the ratio is ~590×. Recommended fix: edit finding to read 'Dome's own H(r) implies ≥38% gravity drop at Antarctic rim under any monotonic coupling (1,430× under linear H(r)→g; ~590× under weakest H^(1/5) coupling)'. Source: WIN-067.c6 hole 3.

WIN-067 detail_evidence asserts 'dome model's stated physics links gravity to aetheric pressure gradient driven by column height H(r)' with no raw-text citation. Domain 4 formula doesn't use H(r) at all. Fix: either (a) cite the specific dome line that ties gravity to H(r), or (b) reframe as IF-THEN conditional ('IF aether pressure depends on H(r) AND gravity depends on aether pressure, THEN...'). Option (b) more defensible. Source: WIN-067.c6 hole 4.

issues.description: WIN-040 c3 H3: The 45°W longitude threshold claim itself is never engaged in detail_evidence. The text debunks WIN-040 only via the generic INTERMAGNET post-hoc / dataset-reuse arguments, never addressing why 45°W is the stated threshold or whether dome geometry predicts any particular longitude for the western boundary. A defender can say "you ignored the specific prediction."

WIN-040 c3 H4: Missing structural argument about radial symmetry. The dome field equation H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) is radially/axially symmetric — it has no longitude dependence. The claimed 45°W SAA boundary is a longitude-specific feature. This structural mismatch (dome predicts azimuthally uniform field; SAA western boundary is a specific azimuthal feature) is the strongest physics-level argument and is absent from detail_evidence.

WIN-040 c3 H5: Post-hoc/timestamp boilerplate in detail_verdict_text lists irrelevant datasets. The sentence about confirming values cites INTERMAGNET, NOAA, and Tomsk datasets — but Tomsk is not the dataset that measures SAA boundary position (that is SWARM/CHAMP satellite data). Listing Tomsk as confirming evidence for a spatial boundary claim is factually imprecise.

WIN-053 c8 H1: "parameterless surface fit" is technically imprecise. The inserted sentence says "B(r) demotes to a parameterless surface fit" — but the dome's B(r) may have parameters (the exponential decay length and amplitude in H(r) that feed into B(r)). If those count as parameters, the claim is arguable. Either defend "parameterless" more carefully or soften to "zero additional degrees of freedom beyond those already in H(r)".

WIN-053 c8 H2: "the toroidal geometry does no predictive work" is over-strong. A defender can respond: "The toroidal topology DOES predictive work — it predicts no isolated magnetic poles and a continuous field wrapping over the dome." The claim should be softened to "does no predictive work beyond what a dipole-plus-residual model would predict" or similar.
