issues.description: WIN-061 c3 H3: Mechanism description is physically backwards. Our text says "storm-injected particles increase D-region conductivity, lowering cavity Q-factor and reducing amplitude." But Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 measured DECREASED damping (increased Q) and INCREASED frequency. Our mechanism and our cited evidence are inconsistent.
issues.fix_details: Applied EXP-227 two-anchor paragraph replacement to WIN-061 detail_evidence. PATCH A: corrected S&F 1999 direction (cavity-Q enhancement, not suppression) + added Pazos 2019. PATCH B: removed backwards D-region mechanism from V51.1 Update paragraph.

issues.description: WIN-068 c9 H4: WIN-025 retains stale "dome's refraction cascade" and "sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate" language from pre-EXP-222 vocabulary. EXP-222 cross-WIN consistency flag: WIN-025 Section 1.8 cross-reference needs updating to use "Coordinate Collapse" vocabulary used in WIN-068.

WIN-010 c6 H1: Internal contradiction between detail_verdict_text paragraphs 1 and 2. P1 says dome "has no equation linking eclipses to magnetic perturbations" but P2 correctly notes dome publishes delta_Z = FSF × baseline equation. Needs rewrite to acknowledge dome equation existence while noting it uses observation-anchored baseline.

WIN-010 c6 H2: "Clean external test" framing in paragraph 7 mis-identifies discriminator. Should frame as: FSF is confirmed H(r)/r, so the open question is whether 2026 observations track FSF (dome-geometric ratio) vs. independent Sq-based predictions — this IS a discriminating test if FSF is the only free parameter.

SEC-8b.3 c5 H1: Attribution misframing. New double-bind paragraph refers to "The Fail-clause concession that [signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone] would falsify the claim" as if the dome said this, but the Fail clause is OUR codification. Rewrite to own this codification: "Our codified Fail-clause..."

WIN-040 c4: FABRICATED CITATION — Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PNAS 114:3543–3548, DOI 10.1073/pnas.1615340114. Crossref 404. Real paper: Terra-Nova & Amit 2017, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter. 266:39–53, DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002.

WIN-040 c4: FABRICATED CITATION — Pávón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016, Earth-Science Reviews 154:96–125. Not in Crossref. Real paper: Pavón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016, Front. Earth Sci. 4:40, DOI 10.3389/feart.2016.00040.

WIN-040 c4 H3: H(r) mislabeled as "dome's field equation" — H(r) is the firmament-HEIGHT function, not a geomagnetic field equation. Fix label to "dome's primary geometric equation" or "firmament-height function H(r)".
WIN-040 c4 H4: Cell centre "near 80°W" is too-western. WMM/CHAOS-7 centres at 65–75°W. Companion fix: "~35° east" gap should be "~20–30° east". Diacritical fix: Pávón → Pavón.

WIN-069 c3 H5: Stale finding field — "6.2% RMSE is 10,000× worse than geodetic standards" is the same misleading number as H2. Fix finding to "orders of magnitude worse than sub-meter geodesy" and lead with same-hemisphere > cross-equatorial anomaly.

WIN-069 c3 H2: Quantitative imprecision — "10,000×–250,000× worse" comparison in detail_verdict_text is misleading. Correct ratio vs sub-meter GNSS is ~2.5×10^5×; vs millimeter is ~2.5×10^8×. Old labels swapped endpoints.

WIN-053 c9 H1: TLDR factual error — "southern coefficient (C=64,852 nT) matches to within ~3%" is only correct against South Magnetic Pole; symmetric comparison (both vs geographic poles per WMM) shows A=62,376 ~13% above geographic NP (~55,000) and C=64,852 ~18% above geographic SP (~55,000). Neither was fit to geographic poles.
WIN-069 c3 H1: derives_from_dome tag should be true — V13 topology + refractive profile are dome-derived even if parameters are calibrated. detail_verdict_text already says so, tag contradicts text.

WIN-053 c9 H2: P3 asymmetric comparators — "~54,000–56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole" vs "~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole" mix pole types. Should symmetrize: both geographic (~55,000 nT each per WMM2025/IGRF-13), since A and C are both post-EXP-226 compared to geographic poles.

WIN-061 c4 H1: TLDR-vs-detail factual contradiction introduced by EXP-227 rewrite. tldr_evidence still asserts backwards mechanism (electron density -> damping cavity resonance) that detail_evidence paragraph 1 now explicitly refutes. tldr_verdict still says "Schumann suppression during storms is standard ionospheric physics" — exact claim the rewritten paragraph 2 says cannot be defended. Reader of collapsed panel sees opposite framing from reader of expanded detail.

issues.description: WIN-061 c4 H5: Solar proton event (SPE) vs geomagnetic storm category conflation in paragraph 1. Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 studied SPEs at Arrival Heights, not CME-driven geomagnetic storms. Pazos et al. 2019 (storm-time enhancement) should lead; S&F should be presented as SPE-specific. Paragraph 2 "most often cited for storm-time SR perturbation" also conflates SPE/storm. Dome defender can argue S&F SPE evidence doesn't rebut G3 storm suppression.
issues.fix_details: EXP-232 patches (SPE/storm category deconflation) applied to WIN-061 detail_evidence. Pazos et al. 2019 promoted to primary storm-time citation; S&F 1999 explicitly qualified as SPE evidence. 2 patches applied, 3449 tests pass.

issues.description: WIN-061 c4 H8 (carry-forward from c3 H7): H_eff averaging method still undisclosed in preserved baseline text ("H_eff ≈ 3,400 km"). Three averaging methods (simple-linear: H_eff=3316 km -> 22.6 Hz; area-weighted: 35.1 Hz; pole-only: 8.79 Hz) all overshoot 7.83 Hz, but text omits method disclosure. Dome defender can claim cherry-picking; trivial to refute but not in text.
issues.fix_details: EXP-233 patch applied: H_eff averaging method disclosure added to WIN-061 detail_evidence. Area-weighted and pole-only alternatives disclosed, plus deductive upper-bound argument (H_eff ≤ 8537 km ⇒ f ≥ 8.79 Hz > 7.83 Hz).

issues.description: WIN-010 c7 H1: P7 reframing introduces asymmetric test setup — dome side has nine specific published FSF numbers (from dome source), globe side uses Kim & Chang 2018 linear magnetic-latitude fit (our construction, not published as predictive tool). A dome defender can argue globe prediction is constructed post-hoc to fit dome values. Need one clarifying sentence showing globe prediction can be computed independently from first principles.

WIN-010 c7 H2: P7 residual formula "(observed delta_Z) / (baseline × coverage)" assumes both models apply coverage as simple multiplicative factor, but the dome FSF is defined geometrically (H(r)/r ratio), not as a linear coverage scaling. If the dome formula is nonlinear in coverage, the common-denominator residual comparison is not apples-to-apples. Need explicit statement of the linear-coverage approximation or verification that dome FSF is linear in coverage.

WIN-025 c3 H1: Missing stronger argument — dome own evolution log admits pre-v45 predecessor Incorrectly predicted 2024 eclipse magnitudes identically across all latitudes. FSF = H(r)/r was introduced specifically to repair that failure. One sentence should be added to detail_verdict_text citing raw-text/06-evolution.txt.

WIN-025 c3 H2: Finding field framing targets 2026 architecture (BOU observation reused as 2026 baseline input) but WIN-025 claims 2024 observation. Second sentence should reframe to lead with WIN-025-relevant critique: dome provides no absolute-magnitude prediction for 2024, observed pattern matches Chapman Sq physics.

WIN-025 c3 H4: Closing triple-count sentence is underdetermined. "WIN-025 shares this mechanism with WIN-010 and WIN-068 — counting the same eclipse geomagnetic observation three times inflates the WIN count without adding independent evidence." Needs to stand on its own post-EXP-229 rewrite: even conceding FSF is genuine, the mechanism (Chapman Sq current disruption) is the same across all three WINs; dome counts one physical process three times at different aggregation scales.

SEC-8b.3 c6 H1: Unscoped overclaim in parenthetical — "so any fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below" is overbroad. A dome defender can argue the ambiguity is a property of OUR chosen Fail-clause wording (ionospheric Sq), not of fair-audit Fail-clauses generally. Scope to "so any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below".

WIN-069 c4 H1: Giacomin & Levinson 2015 citation has wrong journal name. "Transportation Planning and Technology" should be "Environment and Planning B: Planning and Design" (vol. 42, no. 6, 2015). Title is "Road network circuity in metropolitan areas."

WIN-069 c4 H2: Statistic mismatch in Huber/Rust comparison. Terrain models produce sub-1% mean-ratio prediction errors vs V13 6.2% — but V13's 6.2% is a CROSS-EQUATORIAL RMSE, not a mean-ratio error. Comparing mean-ratio to RMSE is not apples-to-apples. Should compare sub-1% mean-ratio to V13 10.2% SAME-HEMISPHERE MEAN ERROR (same statistic).

WIN-068 c11 H2: detail_verdict_text P2 omits coverage term: "every other station predicted delta_Z is a 2017 BOU number scaled by a geometric ratio" should be "scaled by the station obscuration coverage and its FSF = H(r)/r geometric ratio" — matches the 3-factor equation (delta_Z = baseline x coverage x FSF) the c10 finding patch adopted.

WIN-068 c11 H3: Subject ambiguity in triple-count sentence: "WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068 all invoke the same ionospheric Sq mechanism" — "invoke" is ambiguous (dome invokes vs. we invoke?). Rewrite to "all cite observations explained by the same ionospheric Sq mechanism".

WIN-068 c11 H4: Finding says "attaches no dome-derived prediction" which is over-broad — dome DOES attach a dome-derived mechanism claim. Should be "attaches no dome-derived numerical prediction" or "attaches no dome-derived quantitative prediction".

WIN-025 dome-removal annotation missing. The dome author removed WIN-025 (2024 Eclipse 9-Station Confirmation) on 2026-03-15 citing geomagnetic storm contamination. Our wins.json carries it as active but does not annotate the removal. Add a note to detail_evidence so the "refuting a claim already dropped" objection is preempted.

WIN-069 c4 H4 carryover (c3 Hole #2 still open): detail_evidence and TLDRs say V13 errors are "four to five orders of magnitude larger than state-of-the-art geodesy" — but 10,000x is 4 orders, not 4-5. Correct figure is ~250,000x (5 orders of magnitude: sub-meter geodesy vs 6.2% mean errors over thousands of km). Propagate to detail_evidence, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict.
issues.fix_details: EXP-237 applied: 3 patches to WIN-069 (tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_evidence) propagating ~250,000x / five orders of magnitude figure to replace 10,000x / four-to-five orders mislabeling.

WIN-068 c10 H6: Tier-1 Steelman admission sentence appears verbatim in both detail_evidence ¶4 and detail_verdict_text ¶4. Minor dedup cleanup.

WIN-068 c10 H4: detail_evidence ¶2 still uses pre-rename phrasing "the refraction fix leaves physical sun altitude indeterminate" with cross-reference to old section name. Section 1.8 is now "The Coordinate Collapse"; phrasing should be updated.

WIN-025 c4 H5: architectural disjoint between detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text persists (c3 H3 residual). detail_evidence describes WIN-025 purely as Sq-current standard-physics relabeling without introducing the dome scaling equation (delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF) or the BOU anchor import. detail_verdict_text concedes FSF = H(r)/r is genuine geometry and that the BOU absolute magnitude is imported — but the evidence section never set up this architecture, so the verdict concession lands on information the reader has not seen. Requires 2-3 new sentences in detail_evidence after the relabeling paragraph to introduce the scaling equation and BOU anchor, then the verdict text will make sense.

Finding should cite Chapman–Sq framework (Chapman 1919) or Rastogi (1962) with Yamazaki & Maute 2021 for modern conductivity treatment.

Trans. A 218:1-118)" for the foundational Sq work across the cluster, or use the post-1919 family (Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021). Related to ISS-1441 (single WIN) but cluster-wide fix recommended.

WIN-025 still has Chapman 1933 standing alone in corresponding fields, creating cluster citation variance. Needs citation bundle propagated: Chapman 1933 → "Chapman 1919 framework, eclipse-specific 1933 extension" with Rastogi 1962 + Yamazaki & Maute 2021 in detail_evidence and finding.

Recommend dedicated citation-audit EXP to resolve Chapman 1919/1933 venue, Kim & Chang 2018 vol/page, Rastogi 1962 venue, Yamazaki & Maute 2021 DOI in single lookup pass.

WIN-025 c6 H5: tldr_evidence "93 years before the dome claimed it" computed against Chapman 1933 (2026−1933=93), but if H1 propagates Chapman 1919 to WIN-025, prior-art anchor shifts to 107 years. Ambiguity vs WIN-068 (post-ISS-1495) which implicitly sets anchor at Chapman 1919. Defer until H1 cluster-harmonization resolves which year to use.

WIN-067 c10 H1: Pappa et al. 2019 cited as source for BOTH gravity anomaly AND SAA field minimum, but Pappa addresses crustal density structure only; SAA is a core-dynamo phenomenon (reverse-flux patches at CMB, Finlay et al. 2020 IGRF). Defense survives 4/5 on "you are stretching one citation across two physically distinct domains." Needs split: Pappa for gravity, Finlay/Tarduno for SAA. Curmudgeon preemptive rec: reword from "through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure, contribute to the geometry of the SAA field minimum" to separate-citation framing.

Expansion-tracker next_id stale: tracker had next_id=238 while disk held EXP-238 through EXP-242. Also: ISS-1496.assigned_exp="EXP-240" collided with existing EXP-240-WIN069 (Apr 23). Decider should: (1) confirm tracker.next_id is now 244, (2) verify no further allocated EXP IDs are stale, (3) identify root cause of 5-consecutive-miss in tracker updates. proposal-integrity-tracker-disk-drift-1777050094588.json.

WIN-067 c10 H3: The 4.8× Domain-4-violation figure is computed only against the WGS84 polar anchor (9.8322 m/s²) but stated without a range. Should add a range using the three Antarctic-latitude anchors the review uses elsewhere — provides fuller context and is harder for defenders to attack as cherry-picked.

WIN-067 c10 H5: monitoring tag is "none" (retained from c10 baseline) but detail_evidence explicitly cites the formula g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/8619)) from the dome's monitor.py. Tag should be updated to reflect that the formula is monitored/cited, or a note added explaining the "none" tag means no live-fetch (formula is hardcoded).

WIN-067 c10 H6: detail_evidence mixes "V51.0" (ECM model-header version, as printed in 08-live-power.txt L11) with "V51.2" (audit-statistics version, L29) without noting the distinction. Should clarify or unify version references to avoid reader confusion.

WIN-067 c11 H4: λg line-number imprecision in detail_evidence — citation reads "raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 70" but line 70 contains the formula referencing λg symbolically; the numeric value λg=8,619 km is defined at L22. A reader checking L70 finds no value. Fix: change to "lines 22, 70".

WIN-067 c10 H4: closing sentence in detail_evidence reads "The refraction cascade (Section 1.8) adds a further constraint: at the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 15,500–20,015 km), the firmament ceiling H(r) ≈ 1,400–840 km." — announces a constraint but does not deliver it. No follow-through sentence explains what this height range constrains. Needs synthesis sentence completing the argument.

issues.description: WIN-031 c5 H2: Residual advocate-mode defense not preempted. The detail_evidence paragraph on Zaphon argues that ancient Israelites knew about Mt Zaphon and it influenced cosmic-mountain cosmology. A defender can say: "Yes, and the dome model correctly identifies this ANE cosmological cluster — the dome author is using ANE geography, not flat-earth pseudoscience." The preemption (what distinguishes dome cosmology from legitimate ANE scholarship) is not in the text.

issues.description: WIN-013 c3 H2: trilemma clause (c) location-dependent escape closes with "rendering any future null result unfalsifiable" but does not close the subsurface-conductivity defender dodge (Mohe=basement granite, Membach=Devonian carbonate). A sophisticated defender can fill this gap post-hoc.
issues.recommendation: Add sentence after clause (c): "A defender might attribute the difference to subsurface conductivity (basement granite at Mohe vs. Devonian carbonate at Membach), but the dome's published WIN list and monitor.py contain no subsurface-conductivity term, no per-site coupling tensor, and no advance prediction tying expected response to any site-specific geophysical parameter. The escape is post-hoc rationalization, not a model output."

issues.description: WIN-038 c7: "≥5.73 Hz as a lower bound" claim does not mathematically exclude 7.83 Hz — since 5.73 < 7.83, any mode at 7.83 Hz is trivially ≥5.73 Hz. The lower-bound argument closes the dome cavity below 5.73 Hz but does NOT exclude 7.83 Hz from existing. The argument needs reframing: 5.73 Hz is the MINIMUM cutoff, not an upper bound excluding 7.83 Hz. The real exclusion comes from the fact that none of the Bessel modes land at 7.83 Hz, not from the lower bound argument.

issues.description: EXP-252 (Phase B4 verdict-chip click-through): click-handler collision with existing a[href^="#"] global click listener. Inline onclick="goToDefinition();return false" does not stop the site-wide bound handler; results in double history pushState per click and inconsistent scroll position. Fix: either add event.stopImmediatePropagation() to inline handler, or add early-return in the bound listener for .verdict-link elements. Needs change_4 update + new test assertion before integration.
issues.notes: Block EXP-252 integration until resolved. Operator handles generate-html.js integration.
issues.fix_details: Resolved at integration time via design choice rather than code change. The existing global a[href^="#"] click listener (build-scripts/generate-html.js line 1850, attached on window.load) already performs every action goToDefinition() would have performed: find the element by id, locate the containing .ds-tab-content, call showTab(tabId, { skipHash: true, skipScroll: true }), push hash to history, expandToElement() to open ancestor <details>, scrollIntoView({ behavior: smooth }), and call e.preventDefault(). Adding a second inline onclick="goToDefinition();return false" would either duplicate the work (both handlers fire — visible jump) or fight the global handler (return false stops the inline default but the global addEventListener still runs unless we add stopPropagation). Cleanest fix: emit verdict chips as plain <a href="#vl-refuted" class="ds-verdict-link"> with NO inline onclick. The global handler dispatches them correctly via the existing path. EXP-252 application (task #36) will use this no-onclick pattern. ISS-1561 (duplicate :focus-visible CSS) and ISS-1563 (negative-margin click target) remain to address during EXP-252 integration. ISS-1562 (test ratio rewrite) also remains. Closing 1560 with the design decision documented; the actual code application happens in EXP-252.

issues.description: EXP-252 (Phase B4): redundant .verdict-link:focus-visible rule — identical to existing global :focus-visible rule at line 684. Drop the duplicate CSS rule; update test assertion #4 to match.
issues.fix_details: Resolved at integration: dropped EXP-252's .verdict-link:focus-visible CSS rule entirely. The global :focus-visible rule (build-scripts/generate-html.js line 687: outline:2px solid var(--accent);outline-offset:2px;border-radius:3px) already applies to all focusable elements including the new <a class="ds-verdict-link"> wrappers. No per-class override needed. Verified by inspection of generated docs/index.html — only one .ds-verdict-link rule, no :focus-visible variant. Closing.

issues.description: EXP-252 (Phase B4): test assertion #5 uses absolute count (>=60 verdict-link cells) which will break on WIN count changes. Rewrite as ratio check: count td.v-* cells and assert 100% wrap rate instead of absolute number.
issues.fix_details: Resolved at integration: test assertion (e) for EXP-252 wrap coverage uses RATIO check, not absolute count. Computes (td.v-* cells wrapped in ds-verdict-link) / (total td.v-* cells) and asserts >=90%. The 90% threshold (vs the EXP's implicit 60+) leaves headroom for pending predictions which are intentionally unwrapped per PRED_DEF_ID["pending"] === null. Currently 140/148 = 94.6%. Will scale correctly with WIN/prediction count changes. Closing.

issues.description: EXP-252 (Phase B4): negative-margin click-target trick (td .verdict-link margin:-.45rem -.6rem) duplicates td padding values with no test guard. If td padding changes, the click target silently de-fills. Add a test assertion or refactor to CSS variables (--td-pad-y, --td-pad-x) consumed by both td and td .verdict-link.
issues.fix_details: Resolved at integration: added test guard for negative-margin click-target trick. The test extracts td's padding from generate-html.js via regex, computes the expected negative-margin pair (-.45rem -.6rem), and asserts that td .ds-verdict-link's margin equals that negation AND its padding equals td's padding. If td padding ever changes without the link rule being updated in lockstep, this test fails loudly. See test.js EXP-252 block, sub-assertion (f). Closing.

issues.title: EXP-253 §4.2.2: "3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope" is arithmetically ambiguous
issues.description: Closing paragraph uses non-standard phrasing. Reading 1: magnitude is 3.6× the envelope half-width (correct: |−10.9|/3≈3.6). Reading 2: value is 3.6 envelope-widths past the edge ((10.9−3)/3=2.6×). Prose "outside the envelope" nudges toward reading 2. Fix: explicit arithmetic with fraction notation.
issues.patch_hint: Replace "roughly 3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope" with "roughly 3.6 times the falsification half-width (|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6), well outside the dome's own ±3 nT acceptance window"

issues.title: EXP-253 §4.2.2: "replaced with a strawman" is intent-laden; prefer structural verb

Curmudgeon recommends softer structural verb: "misrepresented as zero", "displaced by a zero-baseline", or "treated as zero in the dome's published rule".

issues.description: EXP-254 §4.2.7 does not pre-empt dome's observed vs physical altitude distinction. Dome publishes Sun altitude as (a) 5,733 km (Observation, optical illusion due to aetheric refraction) and (b) <4,200 km (Physical, firmament limit). A defender can argue the section's geometric shadow calc should use physical altitudes, or that aetheric refraction makes geometric shadow inapplicable.
issues.recommendation: Add one paragraph before the Counterargument section: the dome's own physical altitude (<4,200 km) produces an even smaller totality circle (~0.58 km diameter), further from the observed 294 km. Aetheric refraction: the dome offers no ray-tracing or propagation model; a claim that refraction expands the umbra from 1.3 km to 294 km requires an expansion factor of 220 for which no mechanism is given.

issues.description: SEC-4.2.2.c2 H1 (major): §4.2.2 paragraph claims the dome site characterizes the globe prediction as having "no physical mechanism" for eclipse magnetic effects. The dome's predictions.html Tier 1 explicitly cites Sq disruption and WIN-068 honest_caveat. The phrase "no physical mechanism" applies to the gravity tiers (Tier 2/3), not the magnetic Tier 1. This misattribution hands a sophisticated defender a clean rebuttal. Curmudgeon c2 recommends replacing with: acknowledge Sq is cited by dome, then pivot to the structural argument that the operational falsification rule ignores Sq by testing against 0 nT rather than a pre-registered Sq-subtracted residual.

SEC-4.2.2.c2 H2 (moderate): §4.2.2 closing trilemma is missing a fourth horn that a sophisticated defender will use: "the ±3 nT rule applies to the post-Sq residual, not the raw measurement; BOU 2017's −10.9 nT is a raw measurement." Curmudgeon c2 recommends adding a parenthetical or fourth trilemma horn noting the dome owes a published, pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology to make that reading non-arbitrary — without it, "the rule means residual" is a moveable goalpost.

issues.description: EXP-256 falsifiability-module: WCAG contrast not measured for dark-mode. fm-status-num red (--refuted-solid #d46a6a on --card-bg #222240) computes to ~4.43:1 — just below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for normal text (0.95rem bold). fm-status-sub gray (--ink-3 #8a8680 on #222240) ~4.23:1, also below AA. These are pre-existing site-wide token issues, not introduced by EXP-256, but EXP-256 adds new small-size usages.
issues.recommendation: File site-wide token review for --ink-3 dark-mode and --refuted-solid contrast. For EXP-256 specifically: acceptable to land as-is with this tracking issue. If fixing, either bump status-num to 1.05rem+ (large bold = 14pt threshold) or use more saturated red token.

issues.description: EXP-256 falsifiability-module: asymmetric status framing. Condition 3 (Schumann derivation) shows "0/69" in red; condition 4 (coordinates without globe inputs) shows "pending" in gray with OPEN-001. Both have zero demonstrations but different visual treatment. A casual reader will wonder why one is red and one gray without expanding the section.
issues.recommendation: Option A: add brief footer line explaining distinction ("Pending = dome itself flags as unsolved; 0/69 = we evaluated all 69 claims"). Option B: adopt consistent framing — either both "pending" or both "0/69" with sub-status distinguishing them.

issues.description: EXP-254 §4.2.7 curmudgeon c1 H1 (moderate): Section does not pre-empt the "observed vs physical altitude" defensive opening. The dome publishes altitude-of-Sun figures as "observed" not geometric; a defender can claim the dome uses observed altitude corrected for atmospheric refraction so the 1.3 km vs 12,700 km comparison is apples-to-oranges. Section should explicitly close the escape by showing observed altitude doesn't rescue the geometry.

issues.description: EXP-254 §4.2.7 curmudgeon c1 H2 (moderate): The phrasing "dome geometry does not produce a path; it produces a stationary spot under the Moon" is an oversimplification. The dome's Sun moves; the Moon's shadow moves with the Sun. A sophisticated defender can argue the dome predicts a partial-shadow spot that also moves. Rephrase to clarify that the dome's predicted spot diameter is ~1.3 km (nearly stationary on any human timescale of eclipse observation) vs the globe's predicted 160-km wide path.

EXP-254 §4.2.7 curmudgeon c1 H3 (moderate): Lateral velocity of 87 km/min presented as the only figure. The dome author uses angular velocity language (degrees per minute) in his own write-up; presenting only the linear figure that the dome doesn't use lets the dome author claim unit mismatch. Should add equivalent angular velocity (≈0.49°/min as seen from Earth's surface) so the comparison holds in both unit systems.

EXP-254 H2: "The dome geometry does not produce a path; it produces a stationary spot under the Moon" — curmudgeon: this mischaracterizes dome geometry. A dome Moon IS moving; the critique is that it does not produce an ECLIPSE PATH. Reword.
issues.fix_details: Verified content already applied: §4.2.7 does not contain stationary-spot phrasing (ISS-1626) and has upper-bound note for 87 km/min with ~25 km/min mid-disc alternative (ISS-1627). Integration note confirms curmudgeon c1 H2+H3 were applied at integration time.

issues.description: EXP-254 H3: 87 km/min presented as only lateral velocity figure. Curmudgeon: ~25 km/min is more realistic mid-disc velocity. EXP-268 added 25 km/min comparison; verify §4.2.7 parent section also references both velocities.

Add a footnote showing kill survives under alternative convention.
